channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This money-saving emporium is like Facebook Marketplace for gift cards
item.description: You don’t have to be an avid reader of restaurant industry trade publications—though I can attest that they are oddly fascinating—to realize that everything’s getting more expensive. The good news is that there’s an easy way to counteract those rising menu prices.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: The latest DJI Osmo Action 6 image leak gives us our first look at the camera actually in action
item.description: Another picture of the DJI Osmo Action 6 has emerged, and it's likely that more leaks are going to follow.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: How to watch Constellation Cup 2025: free streams, fixture list for Australia vs New Zealand netball series
item.description: All the ways to watch Constellation Cup live streams online for FREE, with the Diamonds on course to win the title outright for the first time in seven years.
item.dc:creator: Aatif Sulleyman, TechRadar

item.title: The end of VPN in Eastern Europe? This massive new subsea cable project looks to bypass Russia entirely - so is this a new age for Internet safety
item.description: Kardesa project seeks safer routes for global data, but true digital independence remains tangled in geopolitics and regional instability.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: How to watch Mayor of Kingstown season 4 online from anywhere – stream Taylor Sheridan crime drama
item.description: Jeremy Renner stars as the "Mayor" looking to stop all out gang war as the hit show returns. Here's how to watch Mayor of Kingstown season 4 online no matter where in the world you are.
item.dc:creator: Tom Wardley, TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI’s company knowledge wants access to all of your internal data
item.description: OpenAI on Thursday rolled out its latest offering, a comprehensive data collection and analysis capability called “company knowledge”.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Zelle plans to expand bank-owned payments network to outside US
item.description: Zelle, the bank-owned service that facilitates consumer money movement, said it plans to expand its services internationally.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: The Bay Area donors that are helping pay for $300 million White House ballroom
item.description: Trump has said the ballroom will be paid for by himself and donors, with no taxpayer money being used.
item.dc:creator: Bay Area News Group, The Mercury News

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In the summers, the sky is jet black when Raul Cruz arrives at this Imperial Valley sugarcane field to start his day. He chops, cleans and bundles the crop, taking heed as the sun rises. It's hard work, but so is starting at 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and JAE C. HONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rivian cuts about 600 jobs in latest setback for EV maker
item.description: Rivian Automotive Inc. is cutting more than 600 jobs, or about 4.5% of its workforce, as the company contends with an unsteady market for electric vehicles in the US.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Intel posts profit even as it struggles to regain market share
item.description: Intel posted profits in the latest quarter as the one-time America technology icon struggles to regain market share.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If someone left a government job with a black eye, literally, ran a company with shrinking profits, and suddenly had federal investigators crawling over their business, you might say they’re having a bad year. But most people are not Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Reddit sues Perplexity, three other firms, for AI scraping
item.description: Reddit this week filed suit against Perplexity and three other companies — Oxylabs UAB, AWM Proxy, and Serp Api — for allegedly engaging in so-called AI scraping without authorization.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Rare dinosaur mummies help scientists recreate their prehistoric lives
item.description: Researchers have unearthed a spooky pair of dinosaur mummies that seem to have been preserved in an unexpected way.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The hidden data problem killing enterprise AI projects
item.description: Headlines alternate between massive AI investments and reports of failed deployments. The pattern is consistent across industries: seemingly promising AI projects that work well in testing environments struggle or fail when deployed in real-world conditions.
item.dc:creator: Brian Moore, Fast Company

item.title: Intel faces high earnings bar as stock soars and cash pours in
item.description: Investors will be looking beyond the bottom line in chipmaker Intel Corp.'s third-quarter earnings after a wave of investments from the White House, Nvidia Corp. and Softbank Group Corp. sent the stock soaring 86% in less than three months.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: How AI can produce detailed storm surge forecasts faster and save lives
item.description: Hurricanes are America’s most destructive natural hazards, causing more deaths and property damage than any other type of disaster. Since 1980, these powerful tropical storms have done more than US$1.5 trillion in damage and killed more than 7,000 people. The No.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Extreme brings AI agent to Platform ONE for swift network diagnostics, troubleshooting
item.description: Extreme Networks has officially worked its Service AI Agent into its flagship network management package, Platform ONE, to give customers rapid access to diagnostics, autonomous troubleshooting, root-cause analysis, dashboards, and automation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Agentic AI – Ongoing coverage of its impact on the enterprise
item.description: Over the next few years, agentic AI is expected to bring not only rapid technological breakthroughs, but a societal transformation, redefining how we live, work and interact with the world. And this shift is happening quickly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A practitioner’s primer on deterministic application modernization
item.description: Large organizations rarely have just a handful of applications. They have thousands, often representing billions of lines of code. These code bases span decades of frameworks, libraries, and shifting best practices.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: American e-waste is causing a 'hidden tsunami' in Southeast Asia, report says
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Millions of tons of discarded electronics from the United States are being shipped overseas, much of it to developing countries in Southeast Asia unprepared to safely handle hazardous waste, according to a new report released Wednesday by an environmental watchdog.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: OpenAI launches Atlas browser to compete with Google Chrome
item.description: OpenAI introduced its own web browser, Atlas, on Tuesday, putting the ChatGPT maker in direct competition with Google as more internet users rely on artificial intelligence to answer their questions.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida test scores rise, attendance improves after cellphone ban
item.description: A new study finds that Florida test scores rose and attendance improved after a school cellphone ban, although short-term disciplinary problems also arose in the state.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: AI’s dark side shows in Gartner’s top predictions for IT orgs
item.description: While AI certainly has the potential to change enterprise operations for the better, it also can present a few “insidious” challenges for IT leaders, according to analysts at the Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo going on this week in Orlando, Fla.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DCIM explained: How data center information management powers AI, capacity planning, and sustainability
item.description: Data center infrastructure management (DCIM) involves managing all aspects of the physical data center. DCIM converges IT management, operations management, facilities management, and automation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI gold rush sparks backlash against Core Scientific acquisition
item.description: CoreWeave’s $9 billion bid for Core Scientific undervalues the data center operator, one of its largest shareholders said Tuesday. It supported the recommendation of investment advisor Institutional Shareholder Services to reject the bid.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: F5 hack highlights persistent supply chain security concerns
item.description: The F5 breach targets a "foundational technology" relied upon by government agencies and critical infrastructure operators. The post F5 hack highlights persistent supply chain security concerns first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI launches web browser ChatGPT Atlas: How to start using it
item.description: OpenAI announced Tuesday it is launching a ChatGPT-powered web browser called Atlas that will compete directly with widely used Google Chrome.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: OPM renews effort to consolidate 119 HR systems into one
item.description: The Office of Personnel Management is looking to implement a commercial software-as-a-service human capital management system for all agencies by July 4, 2027. The post OPM renews effort to consolidate 119 HR systems into one first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Forget blueprints. AI is learning to sketch buildings from scratch, starting with just your words
item.description: "AI will play a significant role in the architectural design process, starting from a supporting tool and eventually becoming a co-designer," Wei Yan said. The post Forget blueprints.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Why cloud and AI projects take longer and how to fix the holdups
item.description: For the last two years, enterprises have been telling me that the failure rate for cloud and AI projects is roughly three times as high as the failure rate for traditional IT projects. And cloud and AI projects take longer than traditional projects.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How Amazon's cloud outage downed much of the internet
item.description: Having a majority of applications and websites rely on a few cloud providers opens up the internet to widespread outages.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: The lost 30 years: The decline of Japan’s semiconductor industry
item.description: Global industrial competition has entered an era in which “whoever controls semiconductors controls the future.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Florida AG issues subpoena to Roblox for child protection policies
item.description: Attorney General James Uthmeier issued a subpoena to a popular online gaming platform, Roblox, on Monday, demanding information about how the platform sets age-verification requirements, moderates chat rooms, and markets itself to children.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: New military age demands new C2 comms
item.description: As purposely scattered forces replace massing, all units need access to rich and latency-free crucial situational data regardless of from where they operate. The post New military age demands new C2 comms first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Anthropic extends Claude Code to browsers
item.description: Anthropic has launched Claude Code on the web, enabling developers to use the company’s AI coding assistant directly from their browser or smartphone, with no terminal required. Developers can connect a first GitHub repository to Claude Code on the web at claude.com/code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: With federal HR in flux, smaller teams are finding smarter ways to do more with less
item.description: "When you do any type of workforce reductions, you have to realize that you're also going to have attrition. Employees are going to leave," Rick Hammell said. The post With federal HR in flux, smaller teams are finding smarter ways to do more with less first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network
